title: Rune Protocol Binding Manifest
description: Canonical binding manifest for Rune Protocol v1.1. Declares all reactive bindings in a host for cross-layer validation. Without this schema, Rune is a convention. With it, Rune is a contract.

$rune.description: Rune Protocol version this manifest conforms to

host.description: Host metadata — the format that owns these bindings

version.description: Version of the host document or application

source.description: Path or identifier of the source document

bindings.description: Named binding declarations. Keys are canonical kebab-case identifiers. Hosts translate to their own conventions — SQL: snake_case, C#: PascalCase, React: camelCase. The schema is the arbiter when they disagree.

BindingDeclaration.description: A single binding declaration. The rune field is the only required property — all others are constraints that validators enforce across layers.

rune.description: The rune type: @ read (state→display, no side effects), ~ sync (state↔input, bidirectional), ! act (user→behavior, explicit trigger), ? intent (annotation only, no runtime effect)

type.description: Value type — validated across all host implementations at build time. Required for @ and ~ bindings.

intent.description: The ? annotation co-located with the binding — rationale, constraint, governance decision. Travels with the binding across all layers and implementations.

args.description: Parameter declarations for ! act bindings. Validators check that call sites pass compatible arguments.

min.description: Minimum value constraint for numeric ~ sync bindings — RNE005 if violated at build time

max.description: Maximum value constraint for numeric ~ sync bindings — RNE005 if violated at build time

enum.description: Allowed values for string ~ sync bindings — RNE005 if an unlisted value is assigned

maxLength.description: Maximum character length for string bindings — matches PIC X(n) in RECALL, VARCHAR(n) in SQL

type.description: Parameter type — 'state-ref' means the argument value is a binding id (the action receives the current state value)

required.description: Whether this parameter must be supplied at call sites
